creator: imp@FreeBSD.org
text:
> For instance, why does the algorithm header uses the macro not provided by
c++98 and not handshaked with the compiler core?

Because it has code to support C++ 03 and earlier that conflicts with sys/cdefs.h by defining static_assert like so

#define static_assert(...) _Static_assert(...)

which causes problems because we also define _Static_assert to be only a 2 argument version and it's used with templates where cpp thinks there's more than 2 arguments...

I take back my earlier patch. I think the real fix is just to kill support for old gcc like so, maybe with the comment from my earlier patch.

-#if !__has_extension(c_static_assert)
 #if (defined(__cplusplus) && __cplusplus >= 201103L) || \
     __has_extension(cxx_static_assert)
 #define        _Static_assert(x, y)    static_assert(x, y)
-#elif __GNUC_PREREQ__(4,6) && !defined(__cplusplus)
-/* Nothing, gcc 4.6 and higher has _Static_assert built-in */
-#elif defined(__COUNTER__)
-#define    _Static_assert(x, y)    __Static_assert(x, __COUNTER__)
-#define    __Static_assert(x, y)   ___Static_assert(x, y)
-#define    ___Static_assert(x, y)  typedef char __assert_ ## y[(x) ? 1 : -1] \
-                           __unused
-#else
-#define    _Static_assert(x, y)    struct __hack
-#endif
 #endif

since (a) has_extension(c_static_assert) seems to be never true in c++ and (b) we don't need support for really old C compilers here, which is what's causing the actual problem here.
